title: Reader API Caching, ETags, Preconditions, and Invalidation · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds privacy-safe conditional reader API from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of privacy-safe conditional reader API while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Classify public, tenant-private, user-private, sensitive, non-storeable, and invalidation-sensitive representations before fields are set.
Derive validators from authorized representation version, query, locale, content negotiation, and principal scope and choose weak or strong semantics.
Evaluate If-None-Match precedence and If-Modified-Since correctly after authentication and representation selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Cache Classification
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 1558 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile private cache classification, ETags, conditional reads, write preconditions, and invalidation; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify public, tenant-private, user-private, sensitive, non-storeable, and invalidation-sensitive representations before fields are set.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Cache Classification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for classify public, tenant-private, user-private, sensitive, non-storeable, and invalidation-sensitive representations before fields are set. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Cache Classification, keep Feed Go Cache Classification connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
State the Feed Go Cache Classification preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
GET responses are safe for shared caches by default. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Workshop Feed Go Invalidation Proof

workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 3177 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test subscribe, ingest, edit, delete, read-state, authorization, locale, and deployment changes against cache keys and validators.
